beat: the post-vibe-coding hangover; codebases are now 40k lines of slop nobody can read
everyone still says they're in 'Founder Mode' but mostly they mean 'I yelled at my CTO today'
every deck claims to be a compound startup; nobody knows what that actually means
AGI is six months away again, for the fourteenth quarter in a row
the solo founder running '47 agents' who has not actually shipped anything
the founder insisting they are 'not a wrapper' while their entire backend is one prompt
the 2am crash-out tweet; the apology Notes-app screenshot by 11am
the 'we're so cooked / we're so back' cycle running on a 90-minute timer
getting publicly ratio'd by an a16z partner before your seed round even closes
founders signing a Cole Valley lease the same week they fail to raise
the TAM slide that multiplies world GDP by 0.4% and calls the assumption conservative
the senior partner who only takes meetings via Twitter DMs and never on his calendar
the demo that worked exactly once on the founder's laptop and has not worked since, including in front of the lead investor
the LinkedIn 'stepping back to spend time with family' post that is unmistakably a board-mandated firing
the $40M Series B announced before a single paying customer, framed as 'stealth design partners'
the 'we're hiring' tweet posted 48 hours after a layoff that nobody is supposed to know about yet
the deck that says 'we are the Stripe of [vertical]' in seven different verticals across seven different decks
